title: Tech
description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from Tech, skipping any feeds with errors.

items.title: General Catalyst commits $5B to India over five years
items.description: The pledge marks a sharp jump from General Catalyst's earlier $500 million–$1 billion India earmark.

items.title: Google’s new Gemini Pro model has record benchmark scores — again
items.description: Gemini 3.1 Pro promises a Google LLM capable of handling more complex forms of work.

items.title: Nvidia deepens early-stage push into India’s AI startup ecosystem
items.description: Nvidia is working with investors, nonprofits, and venture firms to build earlier ties with India's fast-growing AI founder ecosystem.

items.title: Ali Partovi’s Neo looks to upend the accelerator model with low-dilution terms
items.description: Neo's new Residency program invests $750,000 in an uncapped SAFE for startups and provides a $40,000 no-strings-attached grant for college students.

items.title: The 10 Best Shows to Stream Right Now (February 2026)
items.description: Fallout, Neighbors, and Monarch: Legacy of Monsters are just a few of the TV shows we’re swooning over for February.

items.title: Why these startup CEOs don’t think AI will replace human roles
items.description: The CEOs of Read AI and Lucidya told TechCrunch at Web Summit Qatar that they see AI tools replacing tasks, rather than workers.

items.title: Donald Trump Jr.’s Private DC Club Has Mysterious Ties to an Ex-Cop With a Controversial Past
items.description: The Executive Branch has a reported membership list that includes Trumpworld elites like David Sacks. A WIRED review of corporate filings reveals an u...

items.title: Toyota hires seven Agility humanoid robots for Canadian factory
items.description: The robots will be unloading totes full of auto parts from an automated warehouse tugger.

items.title: YouTube’s latest experiment brings its conversational AI tool to TVs
items.description: YouTube is testing conversational AI on smart TVs, allowing viewers to ask the assistant questions related to the video they're watching on the big sc...

items.title: FBI says ATM ‘jackpotting’ attacks are on the rise, and netting hackers millions in stolen cash
items.description: The FBI says hacks that trick ATMs into spitting out cash on demand are rising, with hundreds of attacks in the past year alone netting hackers millio...
